title: Polynomial Freiman–Ruzsa Conjecture
statement_text: The bound in the Bogolyubov–Ruzsa lemma can be improved to $c_r(K) = K^{-O_r(1)}$.
context_text: Theorem 2.3 (Bogolyubov–Ruzsa lemma) states that if $|A+A| \leq K|A|$ in an abelian group of exponent at most $r$, then $2A-2A$ contains a subgroup of size at least $c_r(K)|A|$; the current best bound due to Sanders is $c_r(K) = e^{-O_r(\log^4 2K)}$. The paper notes that the polynomial Freiman–Ruzsa conjecture, described as 'a central open problem in additive combinatorics', would follow from improving this to $c_r(K) = K^{-O_r(1)}$, which would also sharpen the exponent in the paper's Theorem 1.1.
paper_contribution: Uses Sanders's current best bound $c_r(K) = e^{-O_r(\log^4 2K)}$ as an ingredient in the proof of the arithmetic regularity lemma (Theorem 1.1); the conjecture is cited only as motivating context for why the exponent $-d - o(1)$ in Theorem 1.1 might be improvable.
notes: PDF source — the expression 'cr(K) = K−Or(1)' appears garbled; reconstructed as $c_r(K) = K^{-O_r(1)}$ from context. No specific attribution year or citation is given in the paper for the PFR conjecture itself.
paper_title: Efficient arithmetic regularity and removal lemmas for induced bipartite patterns

title: General Induced Arithmetic Removal Lemma [27, Conjecture 5.3]
statement_text: There exists a general induced arithmetic removal lemma.
context_text: The paper observes that the standard reduction from arithmetic removal lemmas to (hyper)graph removal lemmas fails for induced patterns, and states that finding a general induced arithmetic removal lemma 'remains open', citing it as Conjecture 5.3 of reference [27] on arithmetic removal lemmas for linear systems. The present paper establishes such a lemma with polynomial bounds for the special case of bi-induced bipartite patterns in finite abelian groups of bounded exponent.
paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.2, an arithmetic removal lemma with polynomial bounds: if $A \subseteq G$ (finite abelian, exponent at most $r$) is $\varepsilon$-far from bi-induced-$F$-free, then a uniform random map $\phi: V(F) \to G$ bi-induces $F$ with probability at least $\varepsilon^{O(|V(F)|^3)}$, resolving the conjecture for bi-induced bipartite patterns.
notes: Reference [27] is not identified by full citation in the extracted text; the conjecture is cited only as '[27, Conjecture 5.3]'. Statement text is necessarily paraphrased as the precise original formulation is not reproduced in this paper.
paper_title: Efficient arithmetic regularity and removal lemmas for induced bipartite patterns
